summary: Scorching Scarlet>Fire, monster Other One and soaring Bird Song
blurb: The second set erupts with a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue, a marathon 23‑minute Fire jam, and an epic The Other One. Jerry’s guitar tone and vocals are on fire while Phil lays down deep, spacey bass foundations. Highlights also include Bird Song, Samson & Delilah and a heartfelt Knockin' On Heaven's Door.
review: June 19, 1994 at Autzen Stadium captured one of the Dead’s late‑summer high points. The first set wanders through Touch of Grey, Walkin’ Blues and a bright Bird Song but feels uneven compared to the fireworks that follow. Once the band launches into Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain, they never look back: a scorching 23‑minute Fire on the Mountain stretch showcases the rhythm section “up the ante,” while Jerry’s tone is described as perfect and his phrasing emotionally resonant. The second set builds into a monster Other One that rides Phil’s bouncing bass and Vince’s earnest keyboards, eventually spilling into Wharf Rat and Good Lovin' before the encore’s Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door closed the night on an uplifting note. Across three soundboard‑grade recordings reviewers consistently praise Jerry’s soaring vocals and solos, Phil’s deep space‑y intros, Bob’s vocal delivery on Good Lovin', and the dual drumming of Bill and Mickey that drives the long jams. Even with a few first‑set hiccups (Way To Go Home feels flat), the show stands out as a classic 1994 outing with high‑octane second‑set energy and several timeless moments.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, with multiple reviewers calling the performance 'scorching', 'monster', and 'soaring'. Consistent praise for key moments (Scarlet>Fire, Fire on the Mountain jam, The Other One) across sources indicates strong positive sentiment. No significant negative commentary appears.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture; crisp, clear mix with no drop‑outs, delivering the full power of the second‑set jams.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue and a 23‑minute Fire jam
Monster The Other One that rides deep bass and dual drumming
Soaring Bird Song in the first set and heartfelt Knockin' On Heaven's Door encore
High‑energy second set despite an uneven first set

band_performance.Jerry: His guitar tone is described as perfect and his phrasing emotionally resonant; soaring vocals and emotive solos light up the second set.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers deep, space‑y bass intros—especially on The Other One—and bounces around with fun, full‑bodied lines that drive the jams.
band_performance.Bob: Provides strong vocal delivery on Good Lovin' and Knockin' On Heaven's Door and adds cool soundscapes in jam endings.
band_performance.Vince: Keyboard work is noted as less polished than earlier eras but still supports the long improvisations, adding earnest texture throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Together with Bill, he 'ups the ante' on drums, supplying powerful, driving rhythms that propel the marathon jams.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to create a thunderous rhythmic foundation; his drumming fuels the energy of the extended second‑set sequences.
